patterns.verdict_rationale: Lab tier REJECT at lifetime cohort n=5146; outlier event Apr 2026 cluster identified; no mechanism candidate surfaced; no inverse pattern; Section 2b filter from INV-002 does not catch Apr cluster (verified Phase 4)
patterns.hypothesis_primary: UP_TRI breakouts in Bank sector during Choppy regime fail because of [mechanism TBD]. Initial candidate mechanisms (investigation may surface others): (1) sector-internal churn — rate-sensitive flows + index rebalancing absorbing breakout momentum; (2) sub-sector heterogeneity — PSU banks behaving differently from private banks; (3) macro-window proximity — RBI policy / Bank earnings windows distort cohort (e.g., ±3 trading days); (4) volume-divergence — weak-volume breakouts are retail-driven, no institutional follow-through. Investigation tests these candidates plus any additional mechanisms surfaced during Phase 3 cohort comparison (LOSER vs WINNER differentiator analysis).
patterns.hypothesis_inverse: On dates when UP_TRI × Bank × Choppy fails, [pattern TBD] profits. Initial candidate inverse patterns (investigation may surface others): (1) DOWN_TRI × Bank × Choppy on same dates — does shorting the same stocks work? (2) UP_TRI × defensive sectors (Pharma / Energy / FMCG) on same Choppy days — does sector rotation profit? (3) BULL_PROXY (mean-reversion) on same Bank stocks 2-3 days post-failed-breakout — does fade-the-failed-breakout work? Investigation tests these candidates plus any additional inverse patterns surfaced during Phase 3 cross-cohort analysis.
patterns.methodology: 15-year historical backtest (2011-01 → 2026-04); cohort baseline per PROMOTION_PROTOCOL.md Gate 3 thresholds; mechanism analysis (8 differentiators per Gate 5 examples); inverse-pattern search; train/test OOS validation (train 2011-2022 / test 2023-2026); ground-truth validation against /lab/registry/ground_truth_batches/GTB-001.json + GTB-002.json.

live_evidence_at_registration.today_subset: Today's 17 resolutions are SUBSET of the 26 lifetime resolved (not additional): W=2 L=14 F=1 today
live_evidence_at_registration.first_observed: earliest scan_date in cohort per signal_history
live_evidence_at_registration.tier_eligibility_today: Below Tier A n≥50 minimum AND below Tier B n≥30 minimum. Cannot promote to ANY Lab tier on live evidence alone today. Lab investigation expands n via 15-year backtest replay.
live_evidence_at_registration.kill_002_status: Ships separately on main branch as conviction_tag=LIVE_EVIDENCE_ONLY (per Principle 6); not a Lab-tier ship; awaits S-6 prerequisite + INV-001 validation for potential Lab re-promotion.

patterns.expected_outcome: Three paths: (a) Structural failure confirmed + mechanism articulated → kill_002 re-promoted to Lab Tier A or S kill (replacing LIVE_EVIDENCE_ONLY tag); (b) Sub-cohort filter found → kill_002 narrowed to specific Bank sub-sectors; remaining sub-cohorts promoted to boost; (c) Recent-regime artifact → kill_002 remains as live operational rule but does NOT earn Lab tier; investigation pivots to regime classifier review.

patterns.verdict_rationale: Lab parent BOOST tier REJECT; HIGH_VARIANCE_SAMPLE_WINDOW_SUSPECT confirms live n=8 100% WR is sample-window artifact; Section 2b Bank Nifty 60d return is real differentiator (28.26pp Δ WR p<0.001) but does not extend to INV-001 cohort per Phase 4 verification
patterns.hypothesis_primary: UP_TRI × Bank × Bear cohort genuinely profits at near-100% WR due to [mechanism TBD]; n=8 is real signal not statistical artifact. Initial candidate mechanisms (investigation may surface others): (1) Bank stocks oversold during Bear macro; defensive rebound on any breakout signal; (2) rate-cycle correlation — Bear regime often coincides with rate-cut expectations → bank-positive; (3) sample-window bias — Apr 2026 Bear regime was specific 9-day window; cohort may not generalize. Investigation tests for sample-window bias by splitting historical Bear regimes by sub-period and verifying WR holds across multiple Bear episodes (2013, 2016, 2018, 2020, 2022).
patterns.methodology: Backtest infrastructure same as INV-001; cohort baseline 15 years; mechanism analysis; OOS validation. Specifically test for sample-window bias via sub-period split across multiple historical Bear regimes.

live_evidence_at_registration.tier_eligibility_today: Insufficient n for any Lab tier (Tier B minimum n=30; Tier A n=50; Tier S n=100). Lab investigation must establish whether 100% WR is genuine cohort edge or small-sample artifact via 15-year backtest expansion.

patterns.expected_outcome: Three paths: (a) Historical confirmation across multiple Bear episodes → high-confidence Tier S boost (UP_TRI × Bank × Bear with conviction_tag=HIGH); (b) Recent-regime artifact (Apr 2026 Bear was special) → Tier B watch only; flag for re-investigation when next genuine Bear regime arrives; (c) Statistical small-sample noise → reject; document; require larger sample before re-investigation.

patterns.verdict_rationale: 117-cohort scan complete; 0 Tier S, 0 Tier A, 7 Tier B candidates surfaced (3 boost + 4 kill); individual review pending in dedicated session; INV-001 + INV-002 cells reproduced (both REJECT confirms standalone investigations)
patterns.hypothesis_primary: Some sector × regime × signal triples are reliably profitable; others reliably unprofitable. The 99-cohort matrix surfaces tradeable structure not visible from individual investigations.
patterns.methodology: Backtest-driven matrix population. For each of 99 cohort triples (3 signals × 11 sectors × 3 regimes), compute backtest baseline statistics (n, W, L, F, WR, avg pnl, Sharpe, max drawdown). Apply train/test OOS protocol per PROMOTION_PROTOCOL.md Gate 3; rank cohorts by tier eligibility.

live_evidence_at_registration.tier_eligibility_today: N/A — INV-003 is a discovery scan, not a single-cohort hypothesis. Output is the matrix itself; individual cohorts surfaced from the matrix become their own pre-registered investigations.

patterns.expected_outcome: Matrix population: 99 cohorts ranked by tier eligibility. 3-5 high-conviction promotion candidates (Tier A or S boost) identified for individual deeper investigation. 5-10 kill candidates identified for INV-NN follow-ups. Heat-map visualization stored in /lab/analyses/INV-003/cohort_matrix.html.
patterns.note: INV-003 promotes individual cohorts surfaced from the matrix, not the matrix itself. Each surfaced cohort opens its own pre-registered INV-NN.

cohort.filter_proposed: macro_proximity_days within ±3 of RBI MPC announcement

patterns.hypothesis_primary: UP_TRI × Bank × Choppy within ±3 trading days of RBI MPC announcement fails more than baseline. Mechanism: macro uncertainty around policy decisions distorts technical breakout follow-through.
patterns.methodology: Calendar-event tagged backtest. RBI MPC dates pulled from public calendar (8 meetings/year). Bank earnings announcement dates from corporate filings. Tag every UP_TRI × Bank × Choppy signal with macro_proximity_days field; split cohort by proximity bucket; test for WR variance.

live_evidence_at_registration.tier_eligibility_today: Filter pattern; eligibility computed per parent UP_TRI × Bank × Choppy cohort + sub-cohort split. Sub-cohort must independently pass tier requirements per PROMOTION_PROTOCOL.md filter rule.

patterns.expected_outcome: Filter pattern outcome: either (a) macro-proximity filter materially distinguishes win/loss within cohort → graduates as Tier A or B filter pattern; (b) no material distinction → reject; non-filter mechanisms more important.
patterns.note: Low priority; likely won't execute until INV-001..INV-004 complete. Pre-registered for hypothesis discipline.

patterns.verdict_rationale: 12 exit variants × 3 signals tested. UP_TRI × D10: plausibly net positive (+16% capital efficiency, +83% avg pnl, drawdown 2pp deeper at p5; defer migration decision per DECISIONS_LOG). BULL_PROXY × D10: net negative (-6% capital efficiency); stay D6. DOWN_TRI variants invalid due to runner LONG-only logic; deferred to INV-013. Live + backtest infrastructure verified correct; no production fix needed.
patterns.expected_run_session: TBD (own session per signal × variant breakdown)
patterns.hypothesis_primary: D6 exit (current default per scanner.config TARGET_R_MULTIPLE=2.0 + 6-day hold per HOLDING_DAYS) is suboptimal for at least one signal type; alternative exit variants (D2-D10 fixed holds, ATR-based trailing stops, partial-target ladders) yield better risk-adjusted returns. Investigation surfaces optimal exit per signal type via 15-year backtest replay.
patterns.methodology: For each (signal × exit_variant) combination across 15-year backtest: recompute exit point via the variant's rule (fixed hold = exit at OPEN of N-th trading day post-entry; trailing stop = ATR-multiple-below-running-high; ladder = scale out partial size at R-multiples). Compute lifetime stats per cell (n, W, L, F, WR, avg_pnl, Sharpe approximation, max drawdown, profit factor). Apply train/test OOS split (train 2011-2022, test 2023-2026); run hypothesis_tester per (signal × exit_variant) cohort. Compare each variant to D6 baseline; rank by risk-adjusted return.

live_evidence_at_registration.tier_eligibility_today: N/A — INV-006 is a discovery investigation across exit-timing parameter space; no specific live cohort to evaluate. Output is the comparison matrix; individual variants surfaced from the matrix become candidates for adoption (separate Gate 7 user review per variant).

patterns.expected_outcome: Three paths: (a) Single best exit emerges across all signals → switch all signals to that exit; (b) Different optimal exits per signal type → signal-specific exit rules; (c) Current D6 confirmed best → no change. Adoption of any variant requires update to scanner.config (TARGET_R_MULTIPLE / HOLDING_DAYS / new trailing-stop fields) — separate main-branch session.
patterns.note: Pre-flight requires verifying ATR is computed per stock in signal_replayer output (or recoverable from cache parquets via ta-lib / pandas) for trailing-stop variants. If ATR absent, MS-2 may need lightweight extension to add 14-period ATR column; flagged as a sub-task during INV-006 build phase.

patterns.hypothesis_primary: Filtering existing UP_TRI/DOWN_TRI/BULL_PROXY signals by Nifty 20-day volatility percentile yields better tier-eligibility than unfiltered cohorts. Mechanism: vol regime affects breakout follow-through (low-vol consolidation → breakouts run; high-vol whipsaw → breakouts fail) and tail-event frequency (high-vol regime increases STOP_HIT rate).
patterns.methodology: Compute Nifty 20-day rolling vol (std-dev of daily returns × sqrt(252)) across 15-year history. Categorize each signal date by rolling-vol percentile bucket (Low <30%, Medium 30-70%, High >70%) using percentile thresholds computed on the full 15-yr Nifty vol distribution. For each (signal × vol_bucket) of 9 cells, run hypothesis_tester (BOOST + KILL). Compare to unfiltered baseline cohort tier from INV-003 matrix.

live_evidence_at_registration.tier_eligibility_today: N/A — discovery investigation; output is the 9-cell matrix; individual cells become candidates for filter adoption.

patterns.expected_outcome: Three paths: (a) Single vol regime emerges as dominant for all signals (e.g., low-vol-only filter universally improves WR) → universal vol filter; (b) Signal-specific vol preferences (UP_TRI works in low-vol, DOWN_TRI in high-vol) → signal × vol filter combinations; (c) Vol regime adds no edge → filter rejected.

promotion_gate_status.gate_6_sector_specificity: PENDING (vol regime may interact with sector momentum)

patterns.hypothesis_primary: A stock breaking 5-day high with gap-up open AND prior 10-day consolidation has higher WR than UP_TRI base case. Mechanism: gap-up confirms institutional pre-market interest; consolidation prior signals supply absorption; combined → breakout has stronger conviction than UP_TRI alone.
patterns.methodology: Build GAP_BREAKOUT detector implementing the 3-rule signal_definition above; run on 15-year cached parquets (188 stocks). Compute lifetime cohort stats (n, W, L, F, WR, Wilson, p-value); apply train/test OOS split; run hypothesis_tester. Compare to UP_TRI baseline (existing) on same time period + universe. Stratify findings by sector and regime per Gate 6 sector specificity check.

live_evidence_at_registration.tier_eligibility_today: N/A — GAP_BREAKOUT is a NEW signal type not yet in production scanner; no live evidence exists. Investigation establishes whether the signal warrants addition to scanner.scanner_core.

patterns.expected_outcome: Three paths: (a) Tier B/A boost surfaces (lifetime n adequate + WR ≥ tier floor + Wilson + drift bounds) → new signal type to add to mini_scanner_rules and scanner.scanner_core; (b) REJECT → signal definition not edged; archive; (c) Tier eligibility only in specific sectors/regimes → conditional new signal (sector × regime filter built into detector).
patterns.note: Adoption of GAP_BREAKOUT requires extension of scanner.scanner_core.detect_signals + scanner.config new signal type registration + bridge composer signal-routing update. Main-branch ship is separate from Lab promotion call.

candidate_btst_variants.definition: today_close in top 25% of today's range AND today_close > 5_day_high AND today_volume > 1.5 * 20d_avg_volume
stock in sector transitioning Lagging→Leading per sector_momentum (today's bucket = Leading; yesterday's bucket = Lagging or Neutral) AND today_close in top 30% of today's range
stock had UP_TRI W (DAY6_WIN or TARGET_HIT) within past 5 trading days AND has since pulled back 3-5% AND today_close at 3-day high
today's range fully inside yesterday's range (today_high <= yesterday_high AND today_low >= yesterday_low) AND tomorrow's open > today's high

patterns.hypothesis_primary: End-of-day patterns predict next-day open or next-day close strength. Specifically: closing-hour breakout above 5-day high with volume confirmation predicts overnight + next-day continuation. Investigation tests 4 candidate BTST detectors × 3 hold variants = 12 cells; surfaces which (if any) BTST patterns warrant Lab tier eligibility.
patterns.methodology: Build 4 BTST detectors per candidate_btst_variants definitions. For each (BTST_variant × hold_variant) of 12 cells, replay across 15-year cached parquets; compute outcomes per hold rule; lifetime stats; train/test OOS split; hypothesis_tester. Sector + regime stratification per Gate 6. Note: BTST_INSIDE_DAY_BREAKOUT requires forward-looking 'tomorrow's open' which means the signal fires PRE-OPEN tomorrow; entry execution model differs from D6 (entry at open of trigger day, not post-trigger day).

live_evidence_at_registration.tier_eligibility_today: N/A — BTST signals are NEW; no live evidence. Investigation establishes whether any BTST candidate warrants addition to scanner.

patterns.expected_outcome: Three paths: (a) One or more BTST patterns surface as Tier eligible → add to mini_scanner with appropriate hold period (next-day open / next-day close / D2); (b) All BTST patterns REJECT → BTST not viable in this universe; (c) Some BTST patterns work for specific cohorts (sector × regime) → conditional BTST rules.
patterns.note: BTST_INSIDE_DAY_BREAKOUT detection requires next-day open reference; entry model differs from existing D6 LONG signals. Adoption requires bridge L1 PRE_MARKET composer extension to surface BTST entries before market open. BTST signals also carry overnight-gap risk (entry at next-day open, gap-down possible) — risk profile differs from existing intraday signals.

registry_notes: INV-004 is intentionally NOT pre-registered. Its threshold specifics depend on findings from INV-001 mechanism analysis (volume-divergence candidate); pre-registering INV-004 with concrete volume thresholds before INV-001 surfaces evidence would be fitting hypothesis to forthcoming evidence (post-hoc disguised as pre-registration). INV-004 enters this registry once INV-001 completes Phase 3 mechanism analysis. See ROADMAP.md Investigation Queue intro for asymmetry-rationale.

kill_002 is NOT a Lab pattern. kill_002 ships on main branch as conviction_tag=LIVE_EVIDENCE_ONLY based on live signal_history evidence + trader judgment (per Lab Discipline Principle 6). Lab investigation INV-001 may later validate kill_002 against multi-year backtest; if validated, kill_002 may be re-promoted to a Lab tier (S or A) with empirical-cohort tag. Until then, kill_002 is operational-only, not Lab-validated.
